skill.name: zhihu-search-api-skill
skill.description: Search and extract structured Zhihu article data via the BrowserAct API with keyword and date filters.
skill.summary: Automated Zhihu article search and content extraction tool for market research and social listening.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 52 static findings: 46 are false positives (Markdown formatting misidentified as Ruby backtick execution, type specifiers misidentified as weak crypto, documentation URLs and error handling misidentified as reconnaissance). 6 confirmed findings are low-risk and expected for this skill type: the Python script makes authorized HTTP requests to api.browseract.com for Zhihu article search and extraction, and reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables for authentication. No obfuscation, data exfiltration, command injection, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Used for API Credentials
low_findings.description: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables via os.getenv. This is standard practice for API authentication. Credentials should be kept secure and not logged or exposed.

low_findings.title: API Credentials Sent in HTTP Authorization Header
low_findings.description: The skill passes the API key as a Bearer token in Authorization headers to api.browseract.com. This is the intended authentication mechanism for the BrowserAct API.

low_findings.title: External Network Requests to Third-Party API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com for task creation, status polling, and result retrieval. All URLs are hardcoded to the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint.

low_findings.title: Multiple SKILL.md Findings Dismissed as False Positives
low_findings.description: 46 of 52 static findings are dismissed. Markdown code blocks and inline backtick formatting were misidentified as Ruby shell execution (24 findings) and weak crypto (5 findings). Documentation URLs and error handling text were misidentified as system reconnaissance (3 findings) and hardcoded URLs (2 findings). All SKILL.md content is documentation only.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Authentication via External Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines network access with credential access - it reads an API key from environment variables and sends it to an external API. This pattern is expected for API client skills but means users must trust both the skill and the third-party BrowserAct service with their credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Injection Risk Via Dynamic Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill accepts user-provided keywords that are passed as input parameters to the BrowserAct API workflow. While the API call itself is not vulnerable, the SKILL.md instructs the agent to proactively apply itself when users express certain needs, which creates a prompt injection surface if malicious users craft inputs designed to manipulate agent behavior.

content.user_title: Search and Extract Zhihu Articles Automatically
content.value_statement: Manually searching and extracting Zhihu article data is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates the process through the BrowserAct API, returning structured article details and full content with just a keyword.
actual_capabilities: Search Zhihu articles by keyword with configurable date range filters (7d, 30d, 90d, 1y, all)
Extract article title, body content, author, publication date, image URL, and original link
Set extraction limits to control the number of articles returned per search
Monitor task progress with timestamped status logs during execution
Handle errors gracefully with automatic retry logic for transient failures
Detect invalid or expired API keys and provide clear guidance to the user
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable to be set before use
Article extraction tasks may take several minutes due to automated browser operations
Limited to Zhihu content accessible through the BrowserAct workflow API
Automatic retry is limited to one attempt per failed execution

use_cases.title: Industry Trend Tracking
use_cases.description: Search for the latest articles on specific industry topics like AI agents or generative AI to stay current with market developments.

use_cases.title: Brand and PR Monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor how a specific brand or company is discussed on Zhihu over custom time periods to track media exposure and public sentiment.

use_cases.title: Competitor Intelligence Gathering
use_cases.description: Collect structured data about competitor product announcements and market activities published on Zhihu for competitive analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent Zhihu articles about [topic] using the zhihu search API skill. Return the article titles and key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a quick search on a single topic

prompt_templates.title: Filtered Search with Date Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Zhihu for articles about [topic] published in the last [7d/30d/90d/1y]. Extract up to [number] results and summarize the key information from each article.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs results from a specific time window

prompt_templates.title: Search with Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Zhihu search for [topic] using a 7-day window. If the result shows an error that is not Invalid authorization, retry once automatically. If the second attempt also fails, report the specific error to the user.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants robust execution with automatic retry

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Keyword Research Collection
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Zhihu searches for each of these keywords: [keyword1], [keyword2], [keyword3]. Use a 30-day window and limit to 5 articles per search. Compile all results into a research summary organized by topic with article titles, authors, and key takeaways.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs to collect data across multiple topics

output_examples.input: Search for articles about low-altitude economy on Zhihu from the past 7 days
output_examples.output: Returns a list of article titles (such as 'Low-Altitude Economy Market Report 2025'), author names, publication dates, cover image URLs, and direct links. Each result includes the full article body content for reading and analysis.

output_examples.input: Monitor brand mentions for a specific company on Zhihu over the past 30 days
output_examples.output: Structured results showing article headlines, author profiles, exact publication timestamps, main image URLs, and complete article body text for each relevant Zhihu article mentioning the brand.

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill to ensure successful API authentication.
Monitor the timestamped status output during execution to confirm the task is progressing normally rather than stalled.
Choose the appropriate date range filter to balance result freshness against search breadth for your use case.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in scripts or commands. Always use the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Do not ignore Invalid authorization errors. These indicate the API key is expired or incorrect and retries will not help.
Do not assume results are instant. The automated browser workflow may take several minutes to complete execution.

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and how does it work with this skill?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a third-party service that provides automated browser workflows. This skill uses its Zhihu search API template to find and extract article data without manual browsing.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You must set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable with a valid BrowserAct API key before using this skill.

faq.question: How long does article extraction take?
faq.answer: Extraction tasks may take several minutes. The script displays timestamped status logs so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: What data fields are extracted from each article?
faq.answer: Each article result includes title, body content, image URL, author name, publication date, and the original article URL.

faq.question: Can I filter articles by publication date?
faq.answer: Yes. You can filter by past 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, 1 year, or retrieve all articles regardless of date.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid or expired?
faq.answer: The script will detect the Invalid authorization error and stop, guiding you to check and update your API key. It will not retry automatically for authorization errors.
